name: The New Pennsylvania Project
subject: The New Pennsylvania Project Celebrates 2 Years in Communities!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the New Pennsylvania Project
body:
https://secure.everyaction.com/DQ7Hxcpq3Eabel6LA-9V0Q2?refcode=newsletter [https://secure.everyaction.com/DQ7Hxcpq3Eabel6LA-9V0Q2?refcode=newsletter]
Good afternoon, friends, advocates and voters.
We hope this edition of the New Pennsylvania Project eBlast finds you well.
Please take a few moments to check out all the important news you can use, events happening in your neighborhood, career opportunities, and other updates.
The New Pennsylvania Project Celebrates 2 Years in Communities! 🥳🎉
Graphic depicting a tweet from the New Pennsylvania Project on 5/3/23 at 8:47am stating: ''Happy 2 year anniversary to the New Pennsylvania Project. Two years ago today we were a staff of one. Today we have more than 40 Pennsylvanians expanding the electorate by engaging and mobilizing our communities -- year-round! #VotePA #MyTwitterAnniversary'' [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/AV/AV001/1/90920/images/Image-11.jpeg]
Happy 2nd anniversary to the New Pennsylvania Project! Two years ago this week, we were a staff of one with aspirations of expanding the electorate in Pennsylvania, and to center historically disenfranchised Black, Indigenous, and other people of color, immigrant communities and the youth in our work. Since our founding, we have registered more than 20,000 Pennsylvanians to vote.
- 52% of those we registered to vote are under the age of 35
- 66% of registrants self-identified as Black, Indigenous or belonging to another community of color

Welcome New NPP Staff!
Blue Graphic depicting a headshot on the right hand side and text stating: ''Meet Khalif Ali, Director. Khalif joined NPP for the opportunity to strengthen the integrity of our democracy by reinstalling confidence in the practice of democracy. '' [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/AV/AV001/1/90920/images/344577713_785728136274627_1370218906914101774_n.jpg]
NPP CEO, Kadida Kenner, welcomes our newest staffer, Khalif Ali. Khalif joins NPP as the Director.
Meet Khalif Ali! Khalif joins the New Pennsylvania Project as the organization’s Director. Khalif is a Pittsburgh resident with a background in community organizing, engagement and public policy. Khalif works closely with community-based organizations, local government, foundations, and individual activists to craft and advance a progressive policy and advocacy agenda. Vulnerable populations are at the center of his work.
Meet our entire team: https://www.newpaproject.org/npp-team/ [https://www.newpaproject.org/npp-team/] #ExpandTheElectorate #DefendDemocracy.
Tweet of the Week
Screenshot of tweet from the ACLU of Pennsylvania stating: ''We vote because elected officials impact our lives. Learn more about the state of democracy with ACLU-PA's Marian Schneider and Khalif Ali, formerly of Common Cause PA and now with the New Pennsylvania Project. Listen here or wherever you get your podcasts.'' [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/AV/AV001/1/90920/images/IMG_0921%202.jpg]
Pennsylvania’s 2023 primary election is right around the corner, on May 16, and with the delivery of mail and absentee ballots, voting is already underway. So the time is right to hear from Marian Schneider, ACLU-PA’s senior policy counsel for voting rights, and Khalif Ali, formerly of Common Cause PA and now with the New Pennsylvania Project. Marian and Khalif talk about the state of democracy, what challenges we’re facing in protecting the vote, and what opportunities may arise with changes in power at the state capitol.
Listen here [https://www.aclupa.org/en/publications/what-pro-voter-agenda] .
NPP in the News
Last chance to register to vote in Philadelphia primary [https://whyy.org/articles/voter-registration-deadline-philadelphia-primary-elections-2023-may-1/] (WHYY)
Groups like the New Pennsylvania Project have been working to get more voters registered in the city and across the state.
Voter Registration Push [https://www.fox8tv.com/newspost/voter-registration-push/] (Fox8)
News You Can Use
Pa. Democrats want counties to be able to count mail ballots faster. Here’s why changes are unlikely [https://www.inquirer.com/politics/election/pennsylvania-precanvassing-bill-election-vote-count-20230425.html] (Philadelphia Inquirer)
“Newly empowered Pennsylvania House Democrats, in a position to move election legislation for the first time since the 2020 election, are proposing a change to allow mail ballots to be processed earlier so they can be counted faster.
The change is widely backed by elections administrators across the state — but the bill doesn’t have the backing of Republicans, who control the Senate.”
What exactly is ranked-choice voting, and how would it change the Philly mayor’s race? [https://billypenn.com/2023/04/24/ranked-choice-voting-philadelphia-mayor/] (Billy Penn)
The method, which allows voters to — you guessed it — rank their preferred candidates instead of just choosing one, aims to produce more of a consensus winner. It has gained steam nationally in the past few years, but as of now, isn’t legal in Pennsylvania.
After 22 years, LGBTQ+ anti-discrimination legislation clears hurdle in Pa. House [https://www.penncapital-star.com/civil-rights-social-justice/after-22-years-lgbtq-anti-discrimination-legislation-clears-hurdle-in-pa-house/] (PA Capital Star)
The bill, which now goes to the House floor for a vote, would simply add sexual orientation and gender identity or expression to the list of protected classes under the Pennsylvania Human Relations Act.
T he House Judiciary Committee approved the measure on a party-line vote of 12-9.
The proposal inspired emotional and passionate rhetoric from supporters as Republican members of the committee suggested it would infringe on the religious convictions and freedom of individuals and institutions and lead to unintended consequences.
State House gets closer to realizing mail-in ballot and pre-canvassing reforms [https://www.cityandstatepa.com/policy/2023/04/state-house-gets-closer-realizing-mail-ballot-and-pre-canvassing-reforms/385551/] (City & State) [https://www.cityandstatepa.com/policy/2023/04/state-house-gets-closer-realizing-mail-ballot-and-pre-canvassing-reforms/385551/]
Pennsylvania Democrats used the first full week of session since they solidified their majority in the state House to make their priorities clear: They intend to pass long-requested changes to election administration and anti-discrimination laws.
Committees in the House considered dozens of bills this week, and sent more than 40 to the floor for votes by the full chamber. Democrats argue the half-dozen GOP-sponsored proposals they included in that blitz show they intend to run the chamber in a bipartisan way.
House Bill 847 would give counties seven days to pre-canvass ballots – the act of opening, processing and tallying mail-in ballots prior to Election Day. It would also move up the deadline to apply online for a mail-in ballot by four days and allow voters to cure or fix unsigned or undated mail-in ballots.
What will Pa. House Democrats do with a majority? First, they’re moving long-stalled priority bills. [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2023/04/pennsylvania-democrats-house-nondiscrimination-elections-harrisburg/] (Spotlight PA)
Women have more power than ever in Pennsylvania’s legislature, but lawmakers say big hurdles remain [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2023/03/pa-legislature-women-lawmakers-female-representation/] (Spotlight PA)
Women lead both chambers of the state General Assembly, but they still make up less than one-third of the total number of members in the state legislature – a figure that gives Pennsylvania a rank of 27th in the nation for the percentage of women serving in the legislature, Spotlight PA reports.
We're Hiring!
The New Pennsylvania Project is a voting rights organization with a primary focus of registering communities of color and the youth to vote. We are looking for passionate individuals to join our team and help defend democracy by expanding the electorate.

of

and

of

*

*
Fellows
(part-time)
To learn more about how to apply and review job descriptions with requirements and disclosed salary ranges, please click here [https://www.newpaproject.org/careers/] .
Upcoming Events
2023 Appellate Courts Candidates' Forum
When: Tuesday, May 9 6:00pm on Zoom
Pennsylvanians for Modern Courts will host a forum with candidates for Pennsylvania's appellate courts, including the Supreme Court, Superior Court, and Commonwealth Court. This event will provide viewers with an opportunity to get to know the candidates through a moderated forum. Attendees will have the opportunity to submit questions for the candidates.
Register here. [https://us02web.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_4F_dWUz5SMuCxyOC65xqPg#/registration]

Happy 2nd Anniversary to the The New Pennsylvania Project!
Our work has been made possible because of the incredible grassroots support from so many across the Commonwealth and it is about time we all get together and celebrate!
Join us for a fun day at the ballpark to root on the Philadelphia Phillies!

Unidos' 4th Anniversary Community Event in partnership with Resilient LV
When: Saturday, June 10th 12:00 - 3:00pm
Where: Jordan Park

Allentown, PA
Register here [https://www.eventbrite.com/e/humankind-day-summertime-giveaway-healing-event-tickets-532662486837]
Policy Corner
Blue graphic depicting heading as #PolicyCorner with the following words: ''The PA Fairness Act would prohibit discrimination in house, employment and public accommodations on the basis of sexual orientation or gender identity or expression.'' [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/AV/AV001/1/90920/images/NPPEF%2055%20-%20PA%20Fairness%20Act.png]
Did you know Pennsylvania is the only state in the northeast without statewide nondiscrimination protections for the LGBTQIA+ community?
Twenty-four states across the country have extended discrimination protection to members of the LGBTQIA+ community in addition to dozens of counties and municipalities in Pennsylvania.
On Tuesday, May 2, 2023, the Pennsylvania House of Representatives passed an extension of the state’s anti-discrimination law to protect members of the LGBTQIA+ community.
This now moves to the PA Senate to be voted on.
Thank you for taking the time to stay informed. We'll be back in two weeks with the latest edition.
In solidarity,
#TeamNPP

body:
Reliable grassroots commitments early on translate to an even bigger impact down the road.
# [](https://act.lateefahsimon.com/go/39?t=1&akid=23%2E1163%2EQPLKWZ)
In a moment we're going to tell you one of the most powerful ways you can support our movement to send Lateefah to Congress.
As a grassroots-powered campaign, we're constantly planning our budget to maximize efficiency. **The truth is, reliable and early financial investment in our race has a bigger impact than money raised closer to Election Day. Knowing we have a base of reliable, consistent funding is a game-changer for our team.**
**[That's why we're asking our most dedicated supporters (like you ): Will you make a monthly recurring donation of any amount-- even just $5 -- to power our campaign from today through Election Day? ](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ls-em20230505-1&refcode=em20230505-1&recurring=true?refcode2=23_1163_QPLKWZ&akid=23%2E1163%2EQPLKWZ)**
[Donate $5 monthly »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ls-em20230505-1?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=em20230505-1-el&recurring=true&refcode2=23_1163_QPLKWZ&akid=23%2E1163%2EQPLKWZ) [Donate $15 monthly »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ls-em20230505-1?express_lane=true&amount=15&refcode=em20230505-1-el&recurring=true&refcode2=23_1163_QPLKWZ&akid=23%2E1163%2EQPLKWZ) [Donate $25 monthly »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ls-em20230505-1?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=em20230505-1-el&recurring=true&refcode2=23_1163_QPLKWZ&akid=23%2E1163%2EQPLKWZ) [Donate $50 monthly »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ls-em20230505-1?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=em20230505-1-el&recurring=true&refcode2=23_1163_QPLKWZ&akid=23%2E1163%2EQPLKWZ) [Donate $100 monthly »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ls-em20230505-1?express_lane=true&amount=100&refcode=em20230505-1-el&recurring=true&refcode2=23_1163_QPLKWZ&akid=23%2E1163%2EQPLKWZ) [Other amount monthly »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ls-em20230505-1?refcode=em20230505-1&recurring=true&refcode2=23_1163_QPLKWZ&akid=23%2E1163%2EQPLKWZ)
---
Early funding allows us to ensure we reach every voter in California's 12th with Lateefah's message, and will help us start knocking on doors, holding events, and running ads sooner rather than later.
**The California primary is coming sooner than you think,** and this team is ready to out-raise, out-knock, and out-work any candidate that steps into the race in order to keep this seat in progressive hands. **[Will you consider making a monthly contribution of any amount to send Lateefah to Congress so we can be ready for anything that comes our way?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ls-em20230505-1&refcode=em20230505-1&recurring=true?refcode2=23_1163_QPLKWZ&akid=23%2E1163%2EQPLKWZ)**
We really appreciate your commitment to this movement. If you're not ready to make a recurring donation, but want to make a one-time contribution, **[you can do so at this link instead.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ls-em20230505-1&refcode=em20230505-1?refcode2=23_1163_QPLKWZ&akid=23%2E1163%2EQPLKWZ)**
Thank you, thank you THANK YOU!!
With gratitude,
Team Lateefah
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ls-em-wrapper?refcode=em20230505-1&t=2&refcode2=23_1163_QPLKWZ&akid=23%2E1163%2EQPLKWZ)**

* [Update Your Information](https://act.lateefahsimon.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.lateefahsimon.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=23%2E1163%2EQPLKWZ)

Lateefah for Congress

We really appreciate your commitment to this movement. If you're not ready to make a recurring donation, but want to make a one-time contribution, Thank you, thank you THANK YOU!! With gratitude, Team Lateefah

name: Robert Garcia
subject: George Santos is getting out of hand
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Robert Garcia for Congress
body:
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62852279/404793459/938567526?refcode=header&noskip=true&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MjE1NjQzYi03YmViLWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=e-IfzU72uhJp8pcvveap9a9Vekj0YOvXh504qiGcIHA=)
Team,
George Santos is getting out of hand.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62852286/404793466/1270846539?refcode2=7215643b-7beb-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&refcode=em230505-1-t2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MjE1NjQzYi03YmViLWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=e-IfzU72uhJp8pcvveap9a9Vekj0YOvXh504qiGcIHA=)
George Santos wants to divide the LGBTQ+ community. He wants to pit lesbian, gay, and bisexual people against our trans community.
This rhetoric is dangerous.
Not only does Santos not understand how harmful this tweet could be for members of the LGBTQ+ community, but how horribly it could impact young people, who are often more vulnerable and susceptible to bullying and hatred by their peers.
His words aim to divide our community when political and physical attacks against transgender and queer Americans are at a high.
As a gay man myself, I’m disgusted that a member of our community would stoop this low. No one should be posting this kind of hateful content, let alone a member of Congress.
So know that as long as I am serving in Congress, I will continue to fight for everyone in the LGBTQ+ community and continue to hold extremists like George Santos accountable on a national level.
[**If you support me and my work, please make a contribution to my campaign for Congress so I can continue fighting for Americans across the country, regardless of their sexuality or gender identity.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62852291/404793473/1270846539?refcode2=7215643b-7beb-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&refcode=em230505-1-t2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MjE1NjQzYi03YmViLWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=e-IfzU72uhJp8pcvveap9a9Vekj0YOvXh504qiGcIHA=)
Thank you for standing with me in this fight.
Robert Garcia
**[CONTRIBUTE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62852295/404793478/1270846539?refcode2=7215643b-7beb-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&refcode=em230505-1-t2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MjE1NjQzYi03YmViLWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=e-IfzU72uhJp8pcvveap9a9Vekj0YOvXh504qiGcIHA=)**

name: Team Tina
subject: How Tina’s addressing the housing crisis →
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Tina Kotek
body:
Tina for Oregon [https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP12/1/92654/images/TinaForOregon-Logo-FullColor-white.png]
Peter,

Last month, Tina signed the Affordable Housing and Emergency Homelessness Response Package into law — upholding one of her campaign promises to address the housing crisis with the urgency it demands. Let’s break down what’s actually included in the package:

Last month, Tina signed the Affordable Housing and Emergency Homelessness Response Package into law — and she's already putting it into action.

She tied the emergency funding to specific, local plans to rehouse more than 1,200 households and create over 600 new shelter beds in emergency areas by end of the year.

She appointed a diverse group of housing development subject matter experts to the Housing Production Advisory Council, and they are working hard to develop comprehensive recommendations to ramp up home construction across Oregon.

Now, Tina is urging the Legislature to approve another $1.3 billion before this session is over: $1 billion in bonding to build and preserve more affordable housing, and at least $300 million in general funds to continue work on housing and homelessness.

We’re proud of this legislation and grateful to the Oregon legislature for making it a top priority early in session. Tina’s already gotten to work coordinating with state agencies and other stakeholders to put it into action and set Oregon on a brighter path.

Thanks for having Tina’s back now and always. Any progress our team makes is all thanks to your support.

— Team Tina

Tina Kotek is working hard to make Oregon a better place to live and work for all Oregonians – and making real progress on issues like housing and homelessness, education and child care, and mental health and addiction services.
Can you chip in any amount now to support her work?
DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-em-may2023]
Tina for Oregon
PO Box 42307
Portland, OR 97242
tinafororegon.com [https://www.tinafororegon.com/] | info@tinafororegon.com [info@tinafororegon.com]

name: Rashida Tlaib
subject: Abortion pill access restored for now.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress
body:
Abortion is healthcare. | | | [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/ZsYp6Jc2ATNP3MkVzvte6Pmk0F4jS6NOytihmYTOZ1f-yWw6Cj7G9e1dlkX4TL0NMWtZ9DST5cFtdsPS-4D2fKNVbxdzSbLD9T9x2URiTkAkvV0OpQQCoNHFnrXrpjTvrTpsJlmbJZlcFXnbQxd3-TY1T5Q-WakxQCftXots8DxSuIIYbJyEf5dhHT3zH10-rnuR_2geKWhfrwqVifh8gerX_vWTIgaBnQ9CiVV_wJsG_Erv9GPq4qNvPOjoGJ5UL_xfgu5YVQGzJ6WrWSlT-kehRDRMhHjT-wZwzehF3Aw/3vx/jRqymGV4TXSIwfGhDPOnnw/h0/AZkduESh9M1xXOmbYgl3rZxgD1vXIRvJBPNg7BQs3kI)
---
Friend, Right-wing attacks on abortion in the U.S. have escalated, but we have good news for now: **The Supreme Court has temporarily protected access to mifepristone, one of the two drugs that have been proven safe for decades and used for the majority of U.S. abortions.** If you want to find out more about how to access abortion pills, one resource is [https://www.plancpills.org](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDNvGFfyNnCfAkM_HRmVHJAWBBM6eEDRF11tWAnoc87qBQwtUShmmfNpKS3NuHJWtGYoCWRp63V95kZC3MhHz8MDxcOCqoHsFSuOIP_iMVGNvVRyiWqFWfSJ-vnXBpUAgsyTsqgfbPEe7c0FWLNcMLW28_HWIcAgtP1E93k0WKaqVhwMu1gNXmEQpncz70ZrccEuet24s1pP2WzMzsg4gmUeDRG73JiCvtIfsUV7b2u56rFpBneQjJIa2jRYBPLWDJls0nhxQvvBNlANBldEFNWU/3vx/jRqymGV4TXSIwfGhDPOnnw/h1/HWuYv2GzDGBhHoKEEopXleD_znxzcOCy3LaW9p8Bl6k). **I joined so many of you and rallied last week for reproductive justice and I’ll keep fighting for our right to sovereignty over our own bodies—in the U.S. and around the world.** One way to show up for global reproductive rights is by advocating to repeal the Helms Amendment, which has imposed significant barriers to reproductive healthcare worldwide. New legislation does just that. BEGIN HERE**](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=c710adb76fb444579fca3bdf77fd277a&_e=chAUQHwYoQxt6C62hxiIVkGHaUlbpWPLHxgpjULYCY3o3nZUE2aoc5SWl36GvyKEoKsbGk5L_oPLtXpVEuxSAlP6pmc9gQXDEAzDUTHUexUSHxPvrbftGrYn9QIdrdHLY9dP5ZlVPshjz4pLZVW7AMMVW3bLyLy93c2jm4BgG5c4ixA6kQ9WGWAZviL_HVHP1E6KyWAsc90OF0kQO40z4o52uozHSWOqp3KF2qckJv2d3BvvuBb_GhzoKAe8i5ugxwJ3KlM_5ZlzUz7lnQhgHok5QpJ5ju3uYleERj8yiFW_mVdw2aH1RT32Y2j-QKCKlb0SeRdPUavGWvluB_SjBWkcykG54GGOeE5CEVGgK4PvP53WbldUN7-DZKEZ5HEDAl50daZV4A_cszA2DHwfTZUFdao8JWiyPOCRwrZoIPE%3D)

Sheriff Lamb here with a few thoughts on the crisis at the southern border.

President Biden announced earlier this week that he’s sending 1,500 federal troops to the U.S.-Mexico border. I have some thoughts on this, **namely that it didn’t have to come to this.**

The sad part about Biden deploying federal troops to the border is that they’re powerless to help border patrol agents make arrests or process illegal migrants.

We have the facilities to house migrants who break the law, **but they’re sitting empty**.

We have the resources to erect barriers to keep those who want to jump the border at bay, **but they’re rotting in warehouses in the desert.**

As a border-state Democrat, my opponent, Kyrsten Sinema, should be the leading advocate in Washington for border patrol agents and homeland security personnel. Instead, she’s training for marathons and racking up bills dining out in San Francisco, Barcelona, Paris, and Miami. It’s anyone’s guess when Ms. Sinema will tune in – **she has more than $9 million in her campaign accounts, enough to fund her high-flying lifestyle until Election Day and beyond.**

**It's too bad Ms. Sinema doesn’t call it quits and donate the money to the Department of Homeland Security; with $9 million, we could hire 169 new border patrol agents. **

I think Democrats in Washington can – and should – be doing more. But I want to know what conservatives like you think. **Will you do me a quick favor and fill out my border security questionnaire today?** Your participation will be so helpful as we move this Senate campaign forward.

[**SHERIFF LAMB'S BORDER SECURITY SURVEY
BEGIN HERE**](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=c710adb76fb444579fca3bdf77fd277a&_e=chAUQHwYoQxt6C62hxiIVkGHaUlbpWPLHxgpjULYCY3o3nZUE2aoc5SWl36GvyKEoKsbGk5L_oPLtXpVEuxSAlP6pmc9gQXDEAzDUTHUexUSHxPvrbftGrYn9QIdrdHLY9dP5ZlVPshjz4pLZVW7AMMVW3bLyLy93c2jm4BgG5c4ixA6kQ9WGWAZviL_HVHP1E6KyWAsc90OF0kQO40z4o52uozHSWOqp3KF2qckJv2d3BvvuBb_GhzoKAe8i5ugxwJ3KlM_5ZlzUz7lnQhgHpKLHBbnM4f6pYD_xi3p-zzuHSj_gBE216L4NQLJ-R7TVDLeiPNz6xlCfB8Pyi813CrjIE9Nd4ItUysVxeLQRHWgKWiEVRBxddGuQ8ZkRG1rApwF3uRmHEqgWfBeP-kn_botQA3svKXNpIlKKFZgRyQ%3D)
My team and I are reaching out to concerned Americans because **the only way to defeat Democrats and remove them from positions of power is at the ballot box**. When the president of the United States is more concerned about visiting Ireland than he is about visiting the southern border, you know we have a problem.

I’ve said this before and meant it – **and I’m repeating it right now:** as a law enforcement official, the crisis at the border DEEPLY concerns me. There is virtually no way to track the migrants once they enter the United States. Period. We simply don’t have the capacity to handle the influx.

**President Biden knows it. Kamala Harris knows it. And My Democrat opponent certainly knows it**. But none of these folks cared enough about securing the border while we had time on our side. With time having run out, the President is being forced to send federal troops to the border to aid our Border Patrol agents. Shameful.
**
[Thank you for taking the time to share your thoughts with me and my team today. ](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=c710adb76fb444579fca3bdf77fd277a&_e=chAUQHwYoQxt6C62hxiIVkGHaUlbpWPLHxgpjULYCY3o3nZUE2aoc5SWl36GvyKEoKsbGk5L_oPLtXpVEuxSAlP6pmc9gQXDEAzDUTHUexUSHxPvrbftGrYn9QIdrdHLY9dP5ZlVPshjz4pLZVW7AMMVW3bLyLy93c2jm4BgG5c4ixA6kQ9WGWAZviL_HVHP1E6KyWAsc90OF0kQO40z4o52uozHSWOqp3KF2qckJv2d3BvvuBb_GhzoKAe8i5ugxwJ3KlM_5ZlzUz7lnQhgHtqwtKiitSYQ9DbpEMxfrzjXt--u0vct7D6cMRbK-b8QM4iAKdD57knGmPyHvvWx3Tm8QCR2kvEJJWz2dFqTlIXT1qBDOnius7w9q8VqTdGZSpYdGodu_kJesHd_tQqS4t7yqXwuCNBC17lBobFjPtc%3D) **

Sheriff Mark Lamb is a husband, father, grandfather, and God-fearing patriot. From Hawaii to Manila, Arizona, Mark Lamb's rich experirences inspired his life of service. As the 24th Sheriff of Pinal County, Arizona, he drives a powerful agenda to ensure community safety and prosperity.

Sheriff Lamb trained with the Maricopa County Sheriff's Office Deputy Academy and was named Valedictorian of his class. He was hired by the Salt River Pima-Maricopa Indian Community, where he excelled as a police officer and was named Rookie of the Year. The following year, he received the Officer of the Year award. | Team,

**FIRST: Decades of planet-warming fossil fuel emissions caused an extreme rise in global temperatures.

THEN: The impacts of climate change worsened natural disasters like floods, droughts, heat waves, and wildfires.

NOW: Climate change is worsening supply chain turmoil leading to socioeconomic disruptions on a global scale.**

One thing is painfully clear: _**the effects of climate change are not limited to natural disasters.**_

If left unaddressed, climate change will lead to a socioeconomic collapse that could displace thousands and threaten livelihoods everywhere.

NOW: Climate change is worsening supply chain turmoil leading to socioeconomic disruptions on a global scale. body:
We won't let this voter suppression stop us. |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62855532/404802176/1793776944?refcode=bsc_emm_ffa_20230505_f_youngvoters2_v1s3&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZjBiZmM1Yy04MWViLWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=M_ugKhdsPn0RTEBOjfKlU5SCS8iBhdEpz9YbO4ckJmQ=&emci=11b76382-7feb-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&emdi=7f0bfc5c-81eb-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&ceid=1037277)

| | Pure and simple, extremists don’t like it when young people vote. Youth voters turned out in record numbers in the last midterm elections, and their momentum is only getting stronger. At the same time, some political strategists are also trying everything in their power to make it harder for young people and college students to vote. They’ve proposed bills that would ban student ID cards as a form of voter identification, roll back preregistration for teenagers, make absentee voting more difficult for students, and even eliminate college polling places. This coordinated attack on the voting process makes it clear: **keeping young people from voting is part of the anti-democracy playbook.** [_**Fair Fight Action is working alongside young people and college students who are passionate about making their voices heard. But we need your support to ensure that casting a ballot is an easy process for eligible young voters — not an even harder process. If you’re able, can you chip in $20 or anything you can to power this work?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62855536/404802177/2131108340?amount=20&refcode=bsc_emm_ffa_20230505_f_youngvoters2_v1s3&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZjBiZmM1Yy04MWViLWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=M_ugKhdsPn0RTEBOjfKlU5SCS8iBhdEpz9YbO4ckJmQ=) | [CHIP IN](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62855538/404802178/2131108340?amount=20&refcode=bsc_emm_ffa_20230505_f_youngvoters2_v1s3&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZjBiZmM1Yy04MWViLWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=M_ugKhdsPn0RTEBOjfKlU5SCS8iBhdEpz9YbO4ckJmQ=)

Just last month, we saw a surge in youth turnout in Wisconsin for their Supreme Court election. In one of the main polling locations for college freshmen, over 500 voters cast their ballots compared to the 44 votes cast in the same location in 2019. Overall, turnout surpassed 36% of the voting-age population, exceeding the 2011 34% high.
This turnout further illustrates the importance of protecting access to the ballot box for our youth. That’s why Fair Fight Action was proud to deploy our voter engagement task forces alongside partners to defend against extremist attacks and ensure voters and election workers knew their rights in Wisconsin.
Since college students as a demographic are disproportionately affected by anti-voting attacks, we’re advocating for policies like on-campus voting, weekend voting, and expanded hours on college campuses across the country to ensure they can make their voices heard.
[_**Please make a $20 donation today to help ensure we have the resources needed to keep up this work to protect access to the ballot box for our youth in the months ahead.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62855540/404802179/2131108340?amount=20&refcode=bsc_emm_ffa_20230505_f_youngvoters2_v1s3&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZjBiZmM1Yy04MWViLWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=M_ugKhdsPn0RTEBOjfKlU5SCS8iBhdEpz9YbO4ckJmQ=)

clean_body: We won't let this voter suppression stop us. | | | Pure and simple, extremists don’t like it when young people vote. Youth voters turned out in record numbers in the last midterm elections, and their momentum is only getting stronger. At the same time, some political strategists are also trying everything in their power to make it harder for young people and college students to vote. They’ve proposed bills that would ban student ID cards as a form of voter identification, roll back preregistration for teenagers, make absentee voting more difficult for students, and even eliminate college polling places. This coordinated attack on the voting process makes it clear: keeping young people from voting is part of the anti-democracy playbook. = | = Just last month, we saw a surge in youth turnout in Wisconsin for their Supreme Court election. In one of the main polling locations for college freshmen, over 500 voters cast their ballots compared to the 44 votes cast in the same location in 2019. Overall, turnout surpassed 36% of the voting-age population, exceeding the 2011 34% high. This turnout further illustrates the importance of protecting access to the ballot box for our youth. That’s why Fair Fight Action was proud to deploy our voter engagement task forces alongside partners to defend against extremist attacks and ensure voters and election workers knew their rights in Wisconsin. Since college students as a demographic are disproportionately affected by anti-voting attacks, we’re advocating for policies like on-campus voting, weekend voting, and expanded hours on college campuses across the country to ensure they can make their voices heard. = If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will process automatically: = | = ---|--- = | = ---|--- = | = ---|--- Thank you, The Fair Fight Action Team | * 1270 Caroline Street | Suite D120-430 | Atlanta, GA 30307 ©2023 Fair Fight Action - All rights reserved. Emails like this one are the best way to communicate updates with voting rights activists like you. Thank you for being in the fight to ensure access to democracy for all. name: Cliff Schecter, Blue Amp Action
subject: One of our huge midterm victories was winning the Pennsylvania state house by a 101-100 margin. If the GOP takes both seats in the upcoming special election they'll take the majority back and we lose any ability to pass good policy on abortion rights, voting, gun safety, climate, LGBTQ rights and much more, in this crucial swing state.
body:
11 days to PA special elections!
| | | | |
---

| | | _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tba31e95a-7c7f-4567-aec6-541307ce07c9/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

| | | | | Peter, **[In less than two weeks 13 million Americans may be in serious danger, and we can stop it.](https://default.salsalabs.org/Td9650e6c-e009-4cc2-bcef-194fe6d4ee14/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) ** _Some of our biggest midterm victories were in Pennsylvania. Democratic Governor Josh Shapiro. Senator John Fetterman._ **_We even, unexpectedly, took back the state house by a 101-100 margin, _****_creating opportunities to make the lives of 13 million Pennsylvanians better. Yet, what happens in Pennsylvania doesn't stay there--guns, air & water pollution, economic panics like Silicon Valley Bank, don't recognize state lines. _****_With PA the fifth most populous state, bordering big states such as New York, New Jersey & Ohio, what happens there will help or harm a large percentage of Americans._** [_**That's why, with special elections for two open state house seats in PA on May 16th--ONLY 11 DAYS AWAY--we absolutely can't let Repubicans win.**_](https://default.salsalabs.org/T579e19d2-88d6-48ea-b8a1-e4c67394d1e5/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) _**Remember that 101-100 margin? If the GOP takes both seats they'll have a 102-101 margin, we'll lose the state house and with it any ability to pass good policy on abortion rights, voting, gun safety, climate, LGBTQ rights and much more. It'd be a disaster. A direct attack on our liberty.**_ [](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tfaa44cbb-4827-4b96-9911-c6ba84a6c620/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) _Democrat Heather Boyd's been clear that "protecting women's bodily autonomy" is THE reason she's running. Trevor S. Finn's said he'll block permitless carry of guns to protect our children. Both of their GOP opponents will give guns to anyone and ban abortion for everyone. They'll join efforts to attack our democracy._ [**_We can't let this happen, or millions of children, women, Americans in general, will suffer. Will you join Blue Amp in giving $5, $10, $25 or whatever you can to Heather Boyd & Trevor S. Finn to expand our rights, instead of letting MAGA Republicans force policies on us that nearly ended our republic?_**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T16ad170b-61a2-4516-8eb8-30be3e916e19/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) _Thank you for fighting this fight everywhere it's required!_ Cliff

| | | About BlueAmp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! Please follow Cliff Schecter on Twitter @cliffschecter, or subscribe to The Takedown with Cliff Schecter on YouTube, to keep up to date with this battle for the heart and soul of our country. — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T7a5a690a-c1ac-4098-aee3-b08a7bf58e64/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

Peter,

I’m Colin Allred, and I’m running for U.S. Senate to defeat Ted Cruz and deliver better, stronger leadership for the people of Texas.

Winning in Texas will all but guarantee Democrats are able to defend our razor-thin Senate majority – and CNN is already calling Ted Cruz one of the most vulnerable Senators in the country!

**That’s why I need you with me: Please, will you split $10 or whatever you can spare between my campaign and Linda Sánchez to help reach my ambitious Launch Week goal of $2,500,000??_[Every dollar you can spare will help us build a people-powered movement to defeat Ted Cruz, flip Texas blue and defend our Senate majority. I’m a 4th generation Texan, born and raised in Dallas by a single mom. My mom was a public school teacher and sometimes worked two jobs to make ends meet. Thanks to my family and a strong support system of teachers, coaches, and the good folks at the YMCA, I earned a full ride football scholarship to Baylor University and then went on to play in the NFL.

But playing football was never my only dream – from a young age I knew I wanted to help people. So I became a civil rights attorney to defend what I believe is our most fundamental right: the right to vote. I proudly served in President Obama’s administration before I came home to Dallas, where I’m now raising my family.

I ran for Congress because I wanted more kids in North Texas to have the opportunity to achieve their version of the American dream and protect the fundamental values of our democracy – and I beat the odds to defeat an 11-term Republican incumbent, helping Democrats flip the House blue in 2018.

_Now, I’m ready for my next challenge:_ taking on Ted Cruz. I know it won’t be easy, but I also know that Texans deserve so much better than Ted Cruz’s cynical partisan politics. Everything Ted Cruz does is for Ted Cruz – when I’m in the Senate, everything I do will be to help people like you. But I can’t get there alone:

[_**That’s why I’m asking you to chip in right now to become a founding donor of my campaign. We’re going to need an unprecedented grassroots movement to unseat Ted Cruz and I’m counting on you to be a part of it. Please, split $10 or whatever you can spare between my campaign and Linda Sánchez’s to help win in Texas, defeat Ted Cruz and defend our Democratic Senate majority →**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62856269/404802820/-394051622?refcode=BLU-SAN-20230505-1-EM&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85ODI2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NDM5YTczOS04MmViLWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=QlDiWVZ5Wce3IKo4FwodkijzZcxITvZ9TNH5rwPj1Ig=)

Thank you so much for helping me launch this campaign. We have a long way to go, but together, I know we’ve got what it takes to win this thing.

Let’s do this,

Colin Allred

clean_body: Everything Ted Cruz does is for Ted Cruz – when I’m in the Senate, everything I do will be to help people like you. | = Peter, I’m Colin Allred, and I’m running for U.S. Senate to defeat Ted Cruz and deliver better, stronger leadership for the people of Texas. Winning in Texas will all but guarantee Democrats are able to defend our razor-thin Senate majority – and CNN is already calling Ted Cruz one of the most vulnerable Senators in the country! That’s why I need you with me: Please, will you split $10 or whatever you can spare between my campaign and Linda Sánchez to help reach my ambitious Launch Week goal of $2,500,000??= | | | = | = | = I’m a 4th generation Texan, born and raised in Dallas by a single mom. My mom was a public school teacher and sometimes worked two jobs to make ends meet. Thanks to my family and a strong support system of teachers, coaches, and the good folks at the YMCA, I earned a full ride football scholarship to Baylor University and then went on to play in the NFL. But playing football was never my only dream – from a young age I knew I wanted to help people. So I became a civil rights attorney to defend what I believe is our most fundamental right: the right to vote. I proudly served in President Obama’s administration before I came home to Dallas, where I’m now raising my family. I ran for Congress because I wanted more kids in North Texas to have the opportunity to achieve their version of the American dream and protect the fundamental values of our democracy – and I beat the odds to defeat an 11-term Republican incumbent, helping Democrats flip the House blue in 2018. Now, I’m ready for my next challenge: taking on Ted Cruz. I know it won’t be easy, but I also know that Texans deserve so much better than Ted Cruz’s cynical partisan politics. Everything Ted Cruz does is for Ted Cruz – when I’m in the Senate, everything I do will be to help people like you. But I can’t get there alone: = Thank you so much for helping me launch this campaign. We have a long way to go, but together, I know we’ve got what it takes to win this thing. Let’s do this, Colin Allred | = * | | = Paid for by Stand with Sanchez Stand with Sánchez P.O. Box 59660 Norwalk, CA 90652 United States | = = Congresswoman Linda Sánchez has dedicated her life to improving the community where she grew up and making Southern California a better place to live and work for generations to come. # [See ya real soon--in court!](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1137026&post_id=119328773&utm_source=post-email-title&isFreemail=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTE5MzI4NzczLCJpYXQiOjE2ODMzMTg2NzIsImV4cCI6MTY4NTkxMDY3MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTExMzcwMjYiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.r_IG6dwYcd_-8oyMDjdsGsH2FdCWqqmTBPMCVvfu5uo)
### DeSantis and Florida Republicans continue to prove Disney's case
| | [Justin Amash](https://substack.com/redirect/50726639-4968-4e60-8b3f-52428267f076?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/50726639-4968-4e60-8b3f-52428267f076?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1137026&post_id=119328773&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTE5MzI4NzczLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTY4MzMxODY3MiwiZXhwIjoxNjg1OTEwNjcyLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTEzNzAyNiIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.2k5eyHSYzE6blapyl541SJrknG4w6HuVzbYu9V0CMlo&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1137026&post_id=119328773&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTE5MzI4NzczLCJpYXQiOjE2ODMzMTg2NzIsImV4cCI6MTY4NTkxMDY3MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTExMzcwMjYiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.r_IG6dwYcd_-8oyMDjdsGsH2FdCWqqmTBPMCVvfu5uo&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9qdXN0aW5hbWFzaC5zdWJzdGFjay5jb20vcC9zZWUteWEtcmVhbC1zb29uaW4tY291cnQ_dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1zdWJzdGFjayZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJmFjdGlvbj1yZXN0YWNrLWNvbW1lbnQiLCJwIjoxMTkzMjg3NzMsInMiOjExMzcwMjYsImYiOnRydWUsInUiOjkwNTAxMTkyLCJpYXQiOjE2ODMzMTg2NzIsImV4cCI6MTY4NTkxMDY3MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTAiLCJzdWIiOiJsaW5rLXJlZGlyZWN0In0.790opor8tRooxGFx4NnfW-LeGC6rMc1W8FPpN5Wc18c?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1137026&post_id=119328773&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTE5MzI4NzczLCJpYXQiOjE2ODMzMTg2NzIsImV4cCI6MTY4NTkxMDY3MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTExMzcwMjYiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.r_IG6dwYcd_-8oyMDjdsGsH2FdCWqqmTBPMCVvfu5uo)
---
As I've noted on [Twitter](https://substack.com/redirect/ecde5747-0f6e-4b0a-b768-8681c727734b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), the facts and law in the Ron DeSantis versus Disney standoff are strongly on the side of The Walt Disney Company.
Contrary to what we've heard from the Florida governor and Republican officials, Walt Disney World's prior arrangement, in context, wasn't all that remarkable, while the current scheme undeniably treats them unfairly relative to other large amusement parks and businesses. I plan to write a more in-depth piece on this in the near future.
[Share Justin Amash](https://substack.com/redirect/7b9d6ce6-d989-4c39-b142-4887412b7df7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
In the meantime, Governor DeSantis and the Florida Legislature continue to dig a hole for themselves, passing more legislation that makes it clear this entire episode amounts to targeted retaliation for [political speech](https://substack.com/redirect/b886add2-de3d-45e2-9543-a2e59e04d641?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and isn't some principled effort to make everyone live by the same rules.
The latest salvo comes in the form of [HB 1305](https://substack.com/redirect/7bbc8333-ef93-4663-82db-df5b3fa71976?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), a transportation bill incorporating a punitive [amendment](https://substack.com/redirect/5717c740-e19e-43da-83cf-135e97f2e236?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) aimed squarely at the Walt Disney World Monorail System and the Disney Skyliner. The bill, which passed the Senate on Tuesday and the House on Wednesday, barely attempts to disguise its true intentions.
[Existing Florida law](https://substack.com/redirect/fe2324ab-36e8-4401-ac8d-ec3013e257da?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) prescribes minimum safety standards and potential state-conducted safety reviews for [fixed-guideway transportation systems](https://substack.com/redirect/1f960f69-bb98-4338-87a8-f1b0a5a928a8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that are governmentally owned or receive state funding. Using thinly veiled language, the newly passed legislation extends application of these rules to one, and only one, _private_ entity with systems that _aren 't_ financed by state funds: Disney.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/b038a5ab-f279-48a5-b9cd-91d9c81c2520?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
If this amendment were genuinely about public safety, of course, there would be no sense in limiting its application to fixed-guideway transportation systems located within "an independent special district created by local act which have boundaries within two contiguous counties," otherwise known as Walt Disney World.
The legislation also adds a provision stating that any "raised" transportation systems subject to the above standards--which includes both Disney's monorails and gondolas--are also subject to state-conducted structural safety inspections that [previously applied only to bridges](https://substack.com/redirect/27169929-fc66-45a9-af88-0d6566e63a48?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) throughout the state.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e6d19b0e-0b69-409b-ab8d-e9b34417809a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The inclusion of the ambiguous phrase "as appropriate" could mean "as needed for the specific type of structure," but it could also mean that the state may selectively administer these inspections (ahem, cough, Disney). Either way, Disney is the _only_ non-governmentally owned or funded entity that will face routine, perhaps frequent, suspension of system service to accommodate state inspectors, and Disney's primary business in Florida happens to be running the world's busiest theme parks, which depend on fully operational fixed-guideway transportation systems.
Every further action from Governor DeSantis and Florida Republicans seems to prove Disney's case--that this whole saga is about political retribution for constitutionally protected speech. I'll be back soon with a lot more.

body:
Tell the Biden Administration to Protect WOMEN| | | [](http://links.right-gop.com/u/click?_t=2ba4f933732b46f5b6eae32741d1832c&_m=b0da6b3858224533b02123edfda6f612&_e=AFnvPSqym7S9rtjW-fzirxGQeSvpB0oehYQjoophjQWVH-HC5JQ9A0WvUyNyLrjbOfdlB21Qks3cpLyyeZOpOgsWJOowYlJhlZyXfPcDQ3ocTmr1_XXwWxV2_ZuVBhgfuB9Gj2DA_uI1nsMRPoGJe8yYBUUiuckG8KQ7WUoJvwqtXO0ZKhnUTAsQ1rCRK8wfAJUoPp_IneV75GqIjXzzp06-I10nSBC_FacJPYk6CBfSpWjJF50Xa88nChJunSSatNBIL1wp1d2631haRuyHgtcjUU6S_qfMS4K-ovgyG5fp_mtuTpWlxNlW-U1xqX4RIkuAP4_eDCmaVlwkt4qRxJAMvPVHlLfvKuGWRooSUtE%3D)
---
**DEADLINE: MAY 15TH!**
---
Fellow American,

The fight to protect Title IX is CRITICAL to the future of women's sports. I've put my life on the line in the fight to give the next generation of girls more opportunities than I had, but I can't win this fight alone. The Biden Administration is waging a war on women's sports by undermining Title IX. Here's how to help: First, make your voice part of the official record and [**_SUBMIT PUBLIC COMMENT_**](http://links.right-gop.com/u/click?_t=2ba4f933732b46f5b6eae32741d1832c&_m=b0da6b3858224533b02123edfda6f612&_e=AFnvPSqym7S9rtjW-fziry8PfytevtqLGp3cxbTLe7EC_49GsC7pVDXRuQh0e7pzubsIu8aPhV6gcYCBqLi82cVU8v1Ttx0YXtstekO4z39xQsZqQBza79PtGh4avr0PzAtHdSJuLOoGHkl2LTAgQhYe6mTL3_Hg5aNMcYWo_UhEAtmUaSr5YE05iXfdAbNOPUJT_oHhtAydZyGWW1VBWwp2GHvqVnVpHVSTk2tB17WWz56InenooDxV-YxEIZWVxHguiBy4UWyiHFEqYQ8S45Pd_UkHnBnNRgrozez5BHrVcdp33oeKr6bUkA4nw4E2zLZc--Kqc4ZKSxRfH2XIExnaZGj7-6htddJNF9MGxFs%3D) to the Biden Administration. Then [_**DONATE TO NINEPAC**_](http://links.right-gop.com/u/click?_t=2ba4f933732b46f5b6eae32741d1832c&_m=b0da6b3858224533b02123edfda6f612&_e=AFnvPSqym7S9rtjW-fzirxGQeSvpB0oehYQjoophjQWVH-HC5JQ9A0WvUyNyLrjbOfdlB21Qks3cpLyyeZOpOgsWJOowYlJhlZyXfPcDQ3ocTmr1_XXwWxV2_ZuVBhgfuB9Gj2DA_uI1nsMRPoGJe8yYBUUiuckG8KQ7WUoJvwqtXO0ZKhnUTAsQ1rCRK8wfAJUoPp_IneV75GqIjXzzpzp-vg7cuu8rmVJiHJ5Htht3Uj-nk7ikZ77hgvCl-GvK6OGiWJW4iOIdRNSS9v-GEKvw43whgt7teJRE-5KhDUEvv2ZEVa8qz30SHrx8ljRzsEhKUDnTO1Z_7DPbMVUyq73SFUh84wMoJcY0QjK1UEw%3D) to help us FIGHT for women’s sports! As a former college swimmer who lost an NCAA title to a biological MALE, I've experienced the consequences of this erosion of Title IX firsthand. Now the Biden Administration is trying to make it easier for biological males, all at the expense of actual women. The whole point of Title IX was to level the playing field for women, yet woke leftists are turning Title IX on its head by forcing female athletes to compete against biological males. [_**Join my fight to save women’s sports by submitting your public comment—the deadline is May 15th!**_](http://links.right-gop.com/u/click?_t=2ba4f933732b46f5b6eae32741d1832c&_m=b0da6b3858224533b02123edfda6f612&_e=AFnvPSqym7S9rtjW-fziry8PfytevtqLGp3cxbTLe7EC_49GsC7pVDXRuQh0e7pzubsIu8aPhV6gcYCBqLi82cVU8v1Ttx0YXtstekO4z39xQsZqQBza79PtGh4avr0PzAtHdSJuLOoGHkl2LTAgQhYe6mTL3_Hg5aNMcYWo_UhEAtmUaSr5YE05iXfdAbNOPUJT_oHhtAydZyGWW1VBW0jkgSRe1GTleNRkgOsjwDB3cgnorCqUC_uW8m8_t8JpZ6bhzSkP9Ye3k4l3qLkGA95RE7ZIssrl1gCgg3gijiycyi_9uBHav9j5vXJK3pBzgaiSpt_MuVO_ENEkMTZ-L-DNX6BaFpA64EPst3vChWM%3D)
---
[**START YOUR COMMENT**](http://links.right-gop.com/u/click?_t=2ba4f933732b46f5b6eae32741d1832c&_m=b0da6b3858224533b02123edfda6f612&_e=AFnvPSqym7S9rtjW-fziry8PfytevtqLGp3cxbTLe7EC_49GsC7pVDXRuQh0e7pzubsIu8aPhV6gcYCBqLi82cVU8v1Ttx0YXtstekO4z39xQsZqQBza79PtGh4avr0PzAtHdSJuLOoGHkl2LTAgQhYe6mTL3_Hg5aNMcYWo_UhEAtmUaSr5YE05iXfdAbNOPUJT_oHhtAydZyGWW1VBW6ZgY5IPNrD656fhSTejQX5yvYXGpLB5N1mKZWUTOnkNZQQJmAH8Rj00e2O_TJgI9jcSTTKza91iWZBFDCKv60roJvyO57XMuuI-yQ1DLBQhch-OeQi7Naz97JsX_fCT0F4mwr71maf5_Xt8gyq6Mk4%3D)

name: JusticeDemocrats.com
subject: Corruption at SCOTUS
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter, corruption
is running rampant at the Supreme Court.

A new report just came out about a mega-GOP donor and billionaire paying
full tuition for Justice Clarence Thomas’ nephew to attend high-price prep
schools – the same donor who footed the bill for Justice Thomas’ luxury
vacations.

The Supreme Court shouldn’t be a political playground for conservative
billionaires and GOP interests. While SCOTUS slashes away at our
fundamental rights, Supreme Court justices are racking up hundreds of
thousands of dollars from unreported conflicts of interest.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/134312?refcode=em20230505-28811&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1001&akid=28811%2E1203867%2EkMtKtD ]We can’t allow the highest Court in the nation to continue this
corruption – can you contribute $3 now to help us fight
against unchecked corruption and powerful GOP donors?

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/134312?refcode=em20230505-28811&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1020&akid=28811%2E1203867%2EkMtKtD ]Contribute $3

Justice Thomas isn’t the only justice with corrupt ties to GOP donors.
Justice John Roberts’ wife was paid $10 million over an 8-year period for
recruiting top lawyers to elite law firms that argue at the Supreme Court.

Fixing this corruption depends on more than a code of ethics. That’s why
we’re joining forces with other progressives to expand the Court, set term
limits, and make necessary reforms to depoliticize SCOTUS.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/134312?refcode=em20230505-28811&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1021&akid=28811%2E1203867%2EkMtKtD ]We need the resources to fight for these necessary reforms though. Can
you make a contribution now to help us organize for a more just SCOTUS?

We may be up against powerful Republican forces and the entire political
establishment, but we can take them on when we fight back together.

Thanks for all you do,

Justice Democrats

Do not worry if you cannot afford to make a contribution — we understand that
this is a difficult time. If you’re struggling, you can find a food bank
[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/28883?t=1022&akid=28811%2E1203867%2EkMtKtD ]here. We appreciate everything you do to keep our movement strong.

Please stay informed and follow the most up-to-date recommendations from the
[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/24438?t=1023&akid=28811%2E1203867%2EkMtKtD ]Centers for Disease Control and your state public health department.

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20230505-28811-footer&t=1024&refcode2=28811_1203867_kMtKtD&akid=28811%2E1203867%2EkMtKtD

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1025&akid=28811%2E1203867%2EkMtKtD

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
JusticeDemocrats.com
10629 Hardin Valley Rd, #226
Knoxville, TN 37932
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com

[ ]Contribute $3 Justice Thomas isn’t the only justice with corrupt ties to GOP donors. Justice John Roberts’ wife was paid $10 million over an 8-year period for recruiting top lawyers to elite law firms that argue at the Supreme Court. Fixing this corruption depends on more than a code of ethics. That’s why we’re joining forces with other progressives to expand the Court, set term limits, and make necessary reforms to depoliticize SCOTUS. [ ]We need the resources to fight for these necessary reforms though. Can you make a contribution now to help us organize for a more just SCOTUS? We may be up against powerful Republican forces and the entire political establishment, but we can take them on when we fight back together. Thanks for all you do, Justice Democrats Do not worry if you cannot afford to make a contribution — we understand that this is a difficult time. If you’re struggling, you can find a food bank [ ]here. We appreciate everything you do to keep our movement strong. Please stay informed and follow the most up-to-date recommendations from the [ ]Centers for Disease Control and your state public health department. Contribute to Justice Democrats:

name: Lisa Blunt Rochester
subject: my favorite scarf
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Lisa Blunt Rochester for Congress
body:
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62859778/404806668/2120995782?refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2023.05.05_B1_Scarf_Main-90o_logo&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjODhiMTFjMS04NWViLWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WKeUa20ZHhru29bw2cjDCOLPYSu7qsP2pOdcNbGvRIc=) [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62859779/404806669/2120995782?refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2023.05.05_B1_Scarf_Main-90o_logo&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjODhiMTFjMS04NWViLWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WKeUa20ZHhru29bw2cjDCOLPYSu7qsP2pOdcNbGvRIc=) Hey there,

When I was sworn-in for the first time in 2017, my sisters and I wanted to make it special. After all, I was making history as the first woman and person of color elected as the Congressperson for Delaware.

My sister Thea is the historian of the family, and she had found this document from our great-great-great grandfather giving him the right to vote in 1867. Thea is also the crafty one of the family and she kindly printed the document onto a scarf for me. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62859780/404806670/2120995782?refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2023.05.05_B1_Scarf_Main-90o&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjODhiMTFjMS04NWViLWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WKeUa20ZHhru29bw2cjDCOLPYSu7qsP2pOdcNbGvRIc=) The day I was sworn-in, I carried that scarf.

It was surreal – knowing how far my family, and so many families of color, had come. All the way from being enslaved, to finally getting the right to vote, to being elected to represent the First State.

[**I also carried that scarf on the day of the January 6th insurrection.** On such a dark day for our democracy, my scarf served as a reminder that every effort to undermine our democracy has been met with voices of power and reason.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62859781/404806671/2120995782?refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2023.05.05_B1_Scarf_Main-90o&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjODhiMTFjMS04NWViLWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WKeUa20ZHhru29bw2cjDCOLPYSu7qsP2pOdcNbGvRIc=)

**Let’s be clear: blatant attempts to spread lies about the integrity of our democracy are rooted in racism. That’s why electing a Congress that is representative of our country is crucial, and people of color are at the forefront of that mission.**

[**Chipping in to my campaign means chipping in to create a truly diverse and inclusive Congress, and I sincerely hope that you’ll get on board:**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62859782/404806672/2120995782?refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2023.05.05_B1_Scarf_Main-90o&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjODhiMTFjMS04NWViLWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WKeUa20ZHhru29bw2cjDCOLPYSu7qsP2pOdcNbGvRIc=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [**$15 »**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62859783/404806673/2120995782?refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2023.05.05_B1_Scarf_Main-90o&express_lane=true&amount=15&refcode2=EL15&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjODhiMTFjMS04NWViLWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WKeUa20ZHhru29bw2cjDCOLPYSu7qsP2pOdcNbGvRIc=)

name: Colin Allred
subject: Will you help me win in Texas?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fetterman for PA
body:
#
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jf-email-20230505-1?refcode=em20230505-1-8363&refcode2=8363_434096_lkpjfv&akid=8363%2E434096%2Elkpjfv)
Derek, I'm Colin Allred, and I'm running for U.S. Senate to defeat Ted Cruz and deliver better, stronger leadership for the people of Texas.
Winning in Texas will all but guarantee Democrats are able to defend our razor-thin Senate majority – and CNN is already calling Ted Cruz one of the most vulnerable Senators in the country!
**That's why I need you with me from day one: Please, will you split $5 or whatever you can spare between my campaign and John Fetterman to help reach my ambitious Launch Week goal of $2,500,000??[Every dollar you can spare will help us build a people-powered movement to flip Texas blue and defend our Senate majority. Please donate now →](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jf-email-20230505-1?refcode=em20230505-1-8363&refcode2=8363_434096_lkpjfv&akid=8363%2E434096%2Elkpjfv)**
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jf-email-20230505-1?amount=5&refcode=em20230505-8363&refcode2=8363_434096_lkpjfv&akid=8363%2E434096%2Elkpjfv)**
I'm a 4th generation Texan, born and raised in Dallas by a single mom. My mom was a public school teacher and sometimes worked two jobs to make ends meet. Thanks to my family and a strong support system of teachers, coaches, and the good folks at the YMCA, I earned a full ride football scholarship to Baylor University and then went on to play in the NFL.
But playing football was never my only dream – from a young age I knew I wanted to help people. So I became a civil rights attorney to defend what I believe is our most fundamental right: the right to vote. I proudly served in President Obama's administration before I came home to Dallas, where I'm now raising my family.
I ran for Congress because I wanted more kids in North Texas to have the opportunity to achieve their version of the American dream and protect the fundamental values of our democracy – and I beat the odds to defeat an 11-term Republican incumbent, helping Democrats flip the House blue in 2018.
_Now, I'm ready for my next challenge_ : representing Texas in the Senate. As a Senator, everything I do will be to help people like you. But I can't get there alone:
**[That's why I'm asking you to chip in right now to become a founding donor of my campaign on day one. We're going to need an unprecedented grassroots movement to win and I'm counting on you to be a part of it. Please, split $5 or whatever you can spare between my campaign and John Fetterman's to help win in Texas and defend our Democratic Senate majority →](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jf-email-20230505-1?refcode=em20230505-1-8363&refcode2=8363_434096_lkpjfv&akid=8363%2E434096%2Elkpjfv)**
Thank you so much for helping me launch this campaign. We have a long way to go, but together, I know we've got what it takes to win this thing.
Let's do this,
Colin Allred

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jf-emails?refcode=em20230505-8363-footer&t=1&refcode2=8363_434096_lkpjfv&akid=8363%2E434096%2Elkpjfv)**
---
[](https://www.facebook.com/JohnFettermanPA/) | [](https://twitter.com/johnfetterman) | [](https://www.instagram.com/johnfetterman/)
Email us: info@johnfetterman.com
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end. Small donors like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Fetterman for PA, PO Box 6061, Pittsburgh PA 15211.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.johnfetterman.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.johnfetterman.com/sign/personal_info/?t=2&akid=8363%2E434096%2Elkpjfv)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.johnfetterman.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=3&akid=8363%2E434096%2Elkpjfv)

Paid for by Fetterman for PA.

it back again because he would likely personally profit if it’s passed.

This bill is an environmental justice disaster that fast-tracks fossil
fuel projects like the Mountain Valley Pipeline at the expense of
frontline communities. It would also shorten timelines for public input of
permit applications under the National Environmental Policy Act (NEPA) and
the Clean Water Act (CWA).

NEPA is a bedrock environmental law that helps ensure federal agencies
fully assess the effects of proposed actions on people and the
environment.

And, now, on the urging of 2016 Hillary Clinton Campaign Chair John
Podesta, President Biden is supporting Manchin’s dirty deal to speed up
the climate crisis — and all this just after approving the disastrous
Willow Project in Alaska.

subject: Ron DeSantis is coming to Wisconsin

body:
[ https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/67?t=1001&akid=257%2E35263%2Eg8RhhA ]TAMMY BALDWIN FOR SENATE

My team just shared some big news with me, Peter: Florida
Governor Ron DeSantis is coming to Wisconsin this weekend to headline a
big fundraiser for the Wisconsin GOP. [ https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/2155?c0=tbe-em-may23.WiYufF&refcode=AU_STB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20230505_PM1_V1_S1&t=1004&akid=257%2E35263%2Eg8RhhA ]Will you rush a donation to help
us keep Republicans like DeSantis from flipping Wisconsin red? I’ll
explain more in just a moment.

name: OcasioCortez.com
subject: A lot of classist, ageist, misogynist, and racist people are mad.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
#
Alexandria is the youngest woman in U.S. history to be elected to Congress. She was a waitress. She is Latina. She comes from a low-income background and is one of just a few working-class people elected to Congress.
That makes a lot of classist, ageist, misogynist, and racist people mad.
But together with your support, we’ve built a movement fighting for transformative change — from Medicare for All to democratizing fundraising power in politics.
[If you’d like to help support our movement, could you donate $3 or anything you can afford right now to help make sure we have the resources to continue to grow this movement for progress?](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20230505.8iDzd9&refcode=em20230505-4275-P_FND_EGR_GFM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=4275%2E2526796%2ECM9kr5)
**[Contribute $3](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20230505.8iDzd9&refcode=em20230505-4275-P_FND_EGR_GFM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&akid=4275%2E2526796%2ECM9kr5)**
AOC and our movement are often labeled “extreme.” Here’s what they think makes us extreme:
* AOC believes in Medicare for All, aka guaranteed healthcare in the United States.
* AOC operates independently from either party’s establishment. So she is willing to challenge Democratic Party leadership and, when necessary, break from the party line.
* Our movement does not believe in late-stage capitalism or that prioritizing the extreme pursuit of profit at any and all human/environmental costs will save us. AOC believes in cooperative economics and cooperative democracy, aka democratic socialism.

This campaign is funded by grassroots, small-dollar donations and she accepts $0 in corporate money (the average donation to this campaign is around $16).
[ If you believe in fighting for a brighter future for all, please contribute $3 today.](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20230505.8iDzd9&refcode=em20230505-4275-P_FND_EGR_GFM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=21&akid=4275%2E2526796%2ECM9kr5)
Thank you so much for your support,
Team AOC

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/aoc-email-20230505?refcode=em20230505-4275-P_FND_EGR_GFM&t=22&refcode2=4275_2526796_CM9kr5&akid=4275%2E2526796%2ECM9kr5)** | **[Merch](https://shop.ocasiocortez.com/?utm_source=em-footer-20230505-4275-P_FND_EGR_GFM&t=23&akid=4275%2E2526796%2ECM9kr5)**
---|---
[](https://www.facebook.com/OcasioCortez/?t=24&akid=4275%2E2526796%2ECM9kr5) | [](https://twitter.com/AOC?t=25&akid=4275%2E2526796%2ECM9kr5) | [](https://www.instagram.com/aoc/?t=26&akid=4275%2E2526796%2ECM9kr5)
---|---|---
Email us: [speak@ocasiocortez.com](mailto: speak@ocasiocortez.com)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end. Small donors like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, PO Box 680080, Corona, NY 11368.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/signup/did-your-information-change/?t=27&akid=4275%2E2526796%2ECM9kr5)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=28&akid=4275%2E2526796%2ECM9kr5)

Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress

On the other hand, I’ve been relieved to work alongside a reasonable and honorable White House with President Joe Biden at the helm. We must keep Joe Biden in charge.

\- Josh

name: Sherrod
subject: will you take just a moment now to share your thoughts with me?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF SHERROD BROWN
body:
| | |
---
Friend, if there’s one thing you should know about me, it’s this:
**I’ve dedicated my life to fighting for the Dignity of Work – the belief that hard work should pay off for everyone, no matter who you are or what kind of work you do.** For me, this means going to bat for working families in Ohio and across the country. It means making sure _your_ __ voice is heard in the halls of Congress and on the Senate floor.
I’m proud of the progress we’ve made over the last couple of years, including passing a historic infrastructure bill and standing up for workers’ rights. **But we’ve still got a long way to go, so as we chart out our next steps, I’m asking top supporters like you to weigh in.**
I’ve always said the best ideas don’t come from Washington – they come from the people we serve. In an ideal world, I’d get to sit down and chat with each and every member of this grassroots team to find out which issues are keeping you up at night, what makes you hopeful about our future, and what you want to see Democrats accomplish next. But since that just isn't possible, inviting you to join my new digital focus group is the next best thing.
[**Please, friend, will you take just a moment to share your thoughts with me? Your input is so important to helping us protect this seat, defend our Democratic Senate majority, and continue making critical progress for working families.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62862623/404809839/-527431202?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=100,50,25,5&code=jtk174-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzY0NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZmZhN2Y4MC04OGViLWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UnzTIEVy7ZXI-iUGMze3SahW-Fv7aN55W4yKvGefyBA=&emci=3776ad9f-06e9-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&emdi=2ffa7f80-88eb-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&ceid=18332626)
| | [Start the Focus Group >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62862629/404809841/-527431202?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=100,50,25,5&code=jtk174-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzY0NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZmZhN2Y4MC04OGViLWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UnzTIEVy7ZXI-iUGMze3SahW-Fv7aN55W4yKvGefyBA=&emci=3776ad9f-06e9-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&emdi=2ffa7f80-88eb-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&ceid=18332626)
---
Thank you for taking the time. I’m looking forward to reading what you have to say.

With gratitude,

Sherrod
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62862632/404809844/-527431202?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=100,50,25,5&code=jtk174-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzY0NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZmZhN2Y4MC04OGViLWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UnzTIEVy7ZXI-iUGMze3SahW-Fv7aN55W4yKvGefyBA=&emci=3776ad9f-06e9-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&emdi=2ffa7f80-88eb-ed11-8e8b-00224832eb73&ceid=18332626)
| **Please read on for a personal message from Sen. Sherrod Brown: **Friend – thank you for being a part of our grassroots movement and for sharing our commitment to the Dignity of Work.

Fighting for workers means building an economy that prioritizes the needs of workers over tax breaks for billionaires and corporations. No matter who you are or what kind of work you do, I promise to fight for you. But I need your help: Experts say I'm one of the most vulnerable senators up for reelection and Senate control could come down to Ohio. The only way we can defend this critical seat is with grassroots contributions from supporters like you.

**[If you're with me in this fight, please chip in any amount today to win in Ohio and defend our Democratic Senate majority.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/62862633/404809845/-1256022338?refcode=email-footer&code=jtk174-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzY0NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZmZhN2Y4MC04OGViLWVkMTEtOGU4Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UnzTIEVy7ZXI-iUGMze3SahW-Fv7aN55W4yKvGefyBA=)**

clean_body: | | | Friend, if there’s one thing you should know about me, it’s this: I’ve dedicated my life to fighting for the Dignity of Work – the belief that hard work should pay off for everyone, no matter who you are or what kind of work you do. For me, this means going to bat for working families in Ohio and across the country. It means making sure your voice is heard in the halls of Congress and on the Senate floor. I’m proud of the progress we’ve made over the last couple of years, including passing a historic infrastructure bill and standing up for workers’ rights. But we’ve still got a long way to go, so as we chart out our next steps, I’m asking top supporters like you to weigh in. I’ve always said the best ideas don’t come from Washington – they come from the people we serve. In an ideal world, I’d get to sit down and chat with each and every member of this grassroots team to find out which issues are keeping you up at night, what makes you hopeful about our future, and what you want to see Democrats accomplish next. But since that just isn't possible, inviting you to join my new digital focus group is the next best thing. | | Thank you for taking the time. I’m looking forward to reading what you have to say. With gratitude, Sherrod | Please read on for a personal message from Sen. Sherrod Brown: Friend – thank you for being a part of our grassroots movement and for sharing our commitment to the Dignity of Work. Fighting for workers means building an economy that prioritizes the needs of workers over tax breaks for billionaires and corporations. No matter who you are or what kind of work you do, I promise to fight for you. But I need your help: Experts say I'm one of the most vulnerable senators up for reelection and Senate control could come down to Ohio. The only way we can defend this critical seat is with grassroots contributions from supporters like you. = Thank you! = | | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. **OFFICIAL 2023 BORDER SECURITY QUESTIONNAIRE**

**PREPARED FOR:** dpwillis67@gmail.com
